decision.semantic_admission_delta: positive for focused same-file async direct-call Promise continuation fixtures; unchanged on the local crates runtime surface because crates has no JS/TS Promise continuation rows
decision.summary: Open the narrow same-file async-function direct-call producer slice. A direct call to a source-proven async function now carries returned PromiseLike domain evidence, and a pure non-thenable-safe returned payload can feed local Promise.then fulfillment recovery while preserving the Promise boundary.
policy: Async function calls prove only PromiseLike receiver domain from direct call-target evidence plus Source::Protocol(AsyncFunction) evidence.
Fulfilled payload recovery requires the same DirectFunction target proof, arity-safe argument binding, the existing pure-inline sink fence, and a non-thenable-safe returned value.
The recovered value remains behind a Promise boundary and must not merge with synchronous payload code.
Await, throw/rejection, possible thenables, opaque call results, constructor receivers, imported call returns, and dynamic/member call returns remain closed.

source_prevalence.scan_condition: 120-repo JS/TS lexical source scan for .then/.catch/.finally call-return receivers
limitations: The source scan is a pricing signal, not proof of semantic recovery in every source occurrence.
Same-file lexical async names still require normalized DirectFunction evidence before any result-domain proof is emitted.
This slice does not address the larger member/imported call-return receiver queues.

producer_evidence: normalize.call_target.direct_async_function_return_domain emits Domain(PromiseLike) on the call result only when the target root has asserted Source::Protocol(AsyncFunction)
the PromiseLike domain evidence depends on both the DirectFunction target evidence and the async source-protocol evidence
value_graph: direct async-function calls can be inline-evaluated through the existing pure body sink fence
only non-thenable-safe returned payloads become PromiseState::Fulfilled(value)
the Promise.then result remains wrapped in a Promise boundary
newly_supported_equivalences: async function load() { return 1; } load().then(x => x + 1) converges with Promise.resolve(1).then(x => x + 1)
the same recovered Promise continuation stays distinct from synchronous 1 + 1
still_closed_equivalences: async function load() { return await Promise.resolve(1); } load().then(...) does not converge with Promise.resolve(1).then(...)
async function load() { throw 1; } load().catch(...) does not converge with Promise.reject(1).catch(...)
returned possible thenables, explicit PromiseLike values, and opaque call results do not become recovered fulfilled payloads
new Promise(...), imported call-return receivers, member call-return receivers, aggregate combinators, and .finally settlement remain closed
reporting_delta: Promise continuation recall-loss rows no longer report promise-async-function-return-producer-proof when a same-file async DirectFunction receiver carries PromiseLike domain evidence
remaining closed same-file async .then work is attributed to continuation/channel proof, such as promise-then-fulfillment-continuation-contract-missing

covered: DirectFunction calls to source-proven async functions emit PromiseLike domain evidence
the domain evidence depends on both direct callee identity and async source-protocol evidence
same-file async function return recovery converges with Promise.resolve.then
recovery does not erase the Promise boundary into synchronous payload equivalence
await and throw paths remain closed
same-file async receiver producer proof is no longer reported as missing when PromiseLike domain evidence exists
remaining Promise.then work is attributed to fulfillment/rejection/callback obligations

next_work: Recover known local/parameter call-return receivers only when returned PromiseLike domain proof exists for the callee result.
Price imported binding/member call-return receiver proof separately from member selector recovery.
Keep constructor executor, .finally settlement, aggregate combinators, rejection-channel recovery, and broad async/await scheduling closed until their obligations have hard negatives.
